title: Batljan: "Jag har varit Sveriges fattigaste person"
summary_md:
# Batljan: "Jag har varit Sveriges fattigaste person"

**Feed**: dagen-med-di  
**Published**: 2026-05-20  
**Market Sentiment**: Mixed

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

An interview with Ilja Batljan, investment chief at Public Property Invest (PPI), framed around PPI's listing as the largest-ever real estate IPO on Nasdaq Stockholm. Batljan reflects on the SBB property crisis (values down 20-40%), the lesson that a strong balance sheet and BBB+ investment-grade rating are essential, and positions PPI for cautious organic growth (2-2.5bn SEK/year) in elderly care, police stations and prisons across the Nordics. He is structurally bearish on offices due to AI-driven job losses over the next decade.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟠 PPI — Public Property Invest (PPI) | **WATCH** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

IPO pitch by investment chief: stable cashflows, strong BBB+ rating, 13.8bn equity raised, demographically-supported elderly care assets; self-interested but coherent thesis.

#### Reasoning

Batljan positions PPI as a deliberately de-risked successor to SBB, having raised 13.8bn SEK equity in December and targeting a strong BBB+ investment-grade rating to avoid the high-yield trap that crippled SBB. Strategy is cautious organic growth of 2-2.5bn/yr without new capital, focused on demographically-supported segments (elderly care in Helsinki/Gothenburg, police stations/prisons in Norway where PPI owns 15%). Source is talking his own book, so conviction is moderate and an SBB/Batljan v

#### Sell Triggers

- if rating slips below BBB+ toward high yield
- if office/vacancy exposure drags net values
- if balance sheet leverage rises
- if persistent SBB/Batljan discount caps valuation

> *"Det är ett bolag med väldigt stabila intäkter och med stark investment grade rating, BBB+."*
